auxiliary.en: providing extra help or support to something main

ex.en: The hospital hired auxiliary staff during the crisis.
ex.ko: 그 병원은 위기 동안 보조 인력을 고용했다.

available.en: able to be obtained, used, or reached

ex.en: This book is available in three languages.
ex.ko: 이 책은 세 가지 언어로 이용 가능하다.

available.en: (of a person) free and able to be contacted or to meet

ex.en: Is the manager available this afternoon?

avenue.en: a wide street in a town or city, especially one lined with trees

ex.en: Their office is on Fifth Avenue.

avenue.en: a way of approaching or dealing with something

ex.en: We should explore every avenue before giving up.
ex.ko: 포기하기 전에 모든 방법을 모색해봐야 한다.

aware.en: having knowledge or realization of something

ex.en: She wasn't aware of the new policy.

awareness.en: knowledge or understanding that something exists or is important

ex.en: The campaign raised awareness about climate change.
ex.ko: 그 캠페인은 기후 변화에 대한 인식을 높였다.

ex.en: That concert was absolutely awesome.

awkward.en: making you feel uncomfortable or embarrassed

ex.en: There was an awkward silence after his joke.
ex.ko: 그의 농담 뒤에 어색한 침묵이 흘렀다.

awkward.en: not graceful, or difficult to use or handle

ex.en: He made an awkward attempt to dance.

babysit.en: look after children while their parents are out

ex.en: Can you babysit my kids on Friday night?
ex.ko: 금요일 밤에 우리 아이들 좀 봐줄 수 있어?

babysitter.en: a person who is paid to look after children while their parents are out

ex.en: We hired a babysitter for Saturday evening.
ex.ko: 우리는 토요일 저녁을 위해 베이비시터를 고용했다.

ex.en: Sitting all day gave her a backache.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 앉아 있어서 그녀는 요통이 생겼다.

backpack.en: a bag that you carry on your back, held on by two straps over your shoulders

ex.en: He packed his backpack for the trip.

backpacker.en: a person who travels or hikes carrying their belongings in a backpack

ex.en: The hostel is popular with backpackers.

backpacking.en: the activity of traveling or hiking while carrying your belongings in a backpack

ex.en: They went backpacking around Southeast Asia.

bacon.en: meat from the back or sides of a pig, salted or smoked

ex.en: She fried some bacon for breakfast.

baggage.en: the bags and cases you take with you when you travel

ex.en: We picked up our baggage at the airport.

baggage.en: unresolved emotional problems or issues from the past that affect your present

ex.en: He came into the relationship with a lot of emotional baggage.
ex.ko: 그는 많은 정서적 짐을 안고 그 관계를 시작했다.

baker.en: a person whose job is to make and sell bread and cakes

ex.en: The baker gets up before dawn every day.
ex.ko: 그 제빵사는 매일 새벽 전에 일어난다.

bakery.en: a shop or place where bread and cakes are made or sold

ex.en: There's a nice bakery around the corner.

balance.en: keep or put something steady so it does not fall

ex.en: She balanced on one foot to put on her shoes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 신발을 신으려고 한쪽 발로 균형을 잡았다.

balance.en: a state in which weight or amount is evenly distributed so nothing falls or moves

ex.en: He lost his balance and fell off the bike.

balance.en: the amount of money left in a bank account

ex.en: I checked my account balance online.

bald.en: having little or no hair on the head

ex.en: My uncle went bald in his thirties.

bald.en: become bald, losing the hair on your head

ex.en: He started balding at a young age.
ex.ko: 그는 어린 나이에 머리가 빠지기 시작했다.

bandage.en: a piece of cloth used to cover and protect an injury

ex.en: The nurse put a bandage on his cut.

bandage.en: wrap a bandage around an injury

ex.en: She bandaged her ankle after the fall.
ex.ko: 그녀는 넘어진 후 발목에 붕대를 감았다.

ex.en: The door closed with a loud bang.

bang.en: hit something hard, making a loud noise

ex.en: He banged his fist on the table.

bang.en: hair that is cut short and hangs over the forehead

ex.en: She got bangs to change her look.

bank account.en: an arrangement with a bank that lets you keep and manage your money there

ex.en: She opened a bank account at eighteen.

bare.en: uncover or reveal something that was hidden

ex.en: The dog bared its teeth at the stranger.
ex.ko: 그 개는 낯선 사람에게 이빨을 드러냈다.

bare.en: not covered by clothing or anything else

ex.en: She walked on the beach with bare feet.
He packed only the bare essentials for the trip.
ex.ko: 그는 여행에 꼭 필요한 최소한의 것만 챙겼다.

ex.en: I barely finished the race.

barman.en: a man who serves drinks at a bar

ex.en: The barman recommended a cocktail.

barrel.en: a large round container, usually made of wood or metal, for storing liquids

ex.en: The wine was aged in oak barrels.

barrel.en: the long metal tube of a gun through which the bullet travels

ex.en: He looked down the barrel of the rifle.

basement.en: a room or floor of a building that is partly or completely below ground level

ex.en: We store old boxes in the basement.

basin.en: a round bowl or sink used for holding water, especially for washing

ex.en: She washed her face at the basin.

basin.en: a large area of land drained by a river and its smaller rivers

ex.en: The Amazon basin is home to countless species.

basis.en: the main reason or principle that something is based on

ex.en: Her decision was made on the basis of experience.

bathe.en: wash your whole body, usually in a bath

ex.en: She bathed the baby every evening.

bathe.en: be covered or surrounded by light or a particular feeling

ex.en: The garden was bathed in golden sunlight.

battle.en: a fight between armed forces during a war

ex.en: Many soldiers died in that battle.

battle.en: a long and difficult struggle to achieve or resist something

ex.en: She won her battle against cancer.

beard.en: hair that grows on a man's chin and cheeks

ex.en: He grew a beard over the winter.

beast.en: a large or dangerous wild animal

ex.en: The forest was home to many wild beasts.
ex.ko: 그 숲에는 많은 야생 짐승들이 살고 있었다.

beat.en: defeat someone in a game, race, or competition

ex.en: Our team beat them 3 to 1.

beat.en: make a repeated rhythmic sound or movement

ex.en: Her heart was beating fast.

beat.en: the main rhythm of a piece of music

ex.en: The song has a strong beat.

beat.en: the regular route that a police officer patrols

ex.en: The officer walked his beat every morning.
ex.ko: 그 경찰관은 매일 아침 순찰 구역을 돌았다.

ex.en: She sang beautifully at the concert.

beaver.en: a large animal with brown fur that builds dams in rivers using branches

ex.en: Beavers build dams across streams.

because of.en: as a result of; on account of

ex.en: The flight was delayed because of the storm.

bedside.en: the area right next to a bed, especially of someone who is sick

ex.en: She sat at her father's bedside all night.
ex.ko: 그녀는 밤새 아버지의 병상 곁을 지켰다.

behalf.en: used to say that someone is acting or speaking for another person

ex.en: I'm writing on behalf of my manager.
ex.ko: 저는 제 매니저를 대신해서 편지를 씁니다.

behave.en: act or conduct yourself in a particular way

ex.en: The children behaved well during the trip.

belief.en: a feeling that something is true or that something exists

ex.en: It's my belief that things will get better.
ex.ko: 상황이 나아질 거라는 게 내 믿음이다.

ex.en: She dedicated the book to her beloved grandmother.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 책을 사랑하는 할머니에게 헌정했다.

beneath.en: in or to a lower position than something

ex.en: They found an old coin beneath the floor.
ex.ko: 그들은 바닥 아래에서 오래된 동전을 발견했다.

benefit.en: receive an advantage or gain from something

ex.en: Students will benefit from the new library.
ex.ko: 학생들은 새 도서관으로부터 혜택을 받을 것이다.

benefit.en: an advantage or good effect that something has

ex.en: Regular exercise has many health benefits.
ex.ko: 규칙적인 운동은 건강에 많은 이점이 있다.

benefit.en: an extra advantage given by an employer, such as insurance or paid leave

ex.en: The job offers good benefits, including health insurance.
ex.ko: 그 일자리는 건강보험을 포함한 좋은 복리후생을 제공한다.

bent.en: curved or not straight, especially because of damage

ex.en: The bicycle wheel was bent after the crash.
ex.ko: 그 자전거 바퀴는 사고 후 휘어졌다.

ex.en: She was bent on winning the competition.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 대회에서 이기기로 굳게 결심했다.

bet.en: risk money on the result of a game, race, or other event

ex.en: He bet fifty dollars on the horse race.

bet.en: be very confident that something is true or will happen

ex.en: I bet it will rain tomorrow.

bilingual.en: able to speak two languages fluently

ex.en: Their children are growing up bilingual.
ex.ko: 그들의 아이들은 두 언어를 하며 자라고 있다.

bin.en: a container for putting rubbish or waste in

ex.en: Please put the paper in the bin.

biochemistry.en: the scientific study of the chemical processes that happen inside living things

ex.en: She majored in biochemistry at university.

biography.en: an account of someone's life written by another person

ex.en: He wrote a biography of the former president.

biology.en: the scientific study of living things

ex.en: She's studying biology at college.

bishop.en: a senior priest in some Christian churches who is in charge of a district

ex.en: The bishop gave a sermon at the cathedral.

bishop.en: a chess piece that moves diagonally

ex.en: He moved his bishop across the board.

bitter.en: having a sharp, unpleasant taste, not sweet

ex.en: The coffee was too bitter for her.

bitter.en: feeling angry and unhappy because of something unfair that happened

ex.en: He felt bitter about losing his job.
ex.ko: 그는 직장을 잃은 것에 대해 억울해했다.

bleed.en: lose blood from your body, especially from an injury

ex.en: His knee was bleeding after the fall.
ex.ko: 그는 넘어진 후 무릎에서 피가 났다.

blend.en: mix different things together to form a single thing

ex.en: Blend the flour with the milk until smooth.
This coffee is a blend of different beans.
ex.ko: 이 커피는 여러 원두를 혼합한 것이다.

bless.en: ask God to protect or favor someone or something

ex.en: The priest blessed the newly married couple.
ex.ko: 그 신부는 갓 결혼한 부부를 축복했다.

blessing.en: a prayer asking for God's help or protection

ex.en: The priest gave his blessing to the newlyweds.

blessing.en: something good that you are grateful for

ex.en: Having such supportive friends is a real blessing.
ex.ko: 그런 든든한 친구들이 있다는 건 정말 축복이다.

ex.en: He has been blind since birth.

blind.cue: 무언가를 못 보거나 인정 안 하려는 (비유)
blind.en: unwilling or unable to notice or understand something

ex.en: She was blind to his faults.

blog.en: a website where someone regularly writes about their opinions or experiences

ex.en: She started a blog about cooking.

blogger.en: a person who writes a blog

ex.en: He works as a full-time blogger.

blush.en: become red in the face because you are embarrassed or shy

ex.en: She blushed when he complimented her.
ex.ko: 그가 그녀를 칭찬하자 그녀는 얼굴을 붉혔다.

boast.en: talk with too much pride about what you have or have done

ex.en: He boasted about his new car.

boast.en: a statement expressing too much pride in yourself

ex.en: His constant boasts annoyed everyone.
ex.ko: 그의 끊임없는 허풍은 모두를 짜증나게 했다.

bold.en: confident and willing to take risks

ex.en: It was a bold decision to quit her job.

bold.en: a style of printed letters with thick, heavy lines

ex.en: Please write the title in bold.

boldly.en: in a confident way, without fear

ex.en: She boldly spoke up in the meeting.

bomb.en: a weapon that explodes and destroys things

ex.en: The building was destroyed by a bomb.
Enemy planes bombed the city at night.

bond.en: a close connection between people, based on shared feelings or experiences

ex.en: They share a strong bond as sisters.

bond.en: a certificate showing that money has been lent to a government or company, to be repaid with interest

ex.en: The government issued new bonds to raise money.
ex.ko: 정부는 자금을 마련하기 위해 새 채권을 발행했다.

booking.en: an arrangement to have a seat, room, or ticket reserved for a future time

ex.en: I made a booking for two at the restaurant.
There was a loud boom as the fireworks went off.
ex.ko: 불꽃놀이가 터지면서 쾅 하는 소리가 났다.

boom.en: a sudden increase in trade, business, or economic activity

ex.en: The country experienced an economic boom in the 1990s.

boot.en: a type of shoe that covers the foot and part of the leg

ex.en: She wore rain boots to walk through the puddles.

boot.en: the covered space at the back of a car for luggage (British English)

ex.en: He put the suitcases in the boot of the car.
ex.ko: 그는 여행 가방들을 차 트렁크에 실었다.

border.en: the line that separates two countries or areas

ex.en: They crossed the border into France.

border.en: the edge or outer part of something

ex.en: The photo had a white border around it.
ex.ko: 그 사진은 주위에 하얀 테두리가 있었다.

bore.en: make someone feel bored, usually by being uninteresting

ex.en: The long lecture bored the students.
ex.ko: 그 긴 강의는 학생들을 지루하게 했다.

bore.en: make a hole in something using a tool

ex.en: Workers bored a tunnel through the mountain.
He sighed with boredom during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그는 회의 동안 지루함에 한숨을 쉬었다.

bounce.en: spring back after hitting a surface

ex.en: The ball bounced off the wall.

bounce.en: the quality of being able to bounce

ex.en: This mattress has good bounce.

bound.en: tied tightly with rope, string, or similar material

ex.en: The prisoner's hands were bound behind his back.
ex.ko: 그 죄수의 손은 등 뒤로 묶여 있었다.

bound.cue: 어떤 일이 일어날 것이 거의 확실한

ex.en: If you don't study, you're bound to fail.
The flight is bound for Tokyo.

bow.en: bend your head or body forward as a sign of respect or greeting

ex.en: He bowed to the audience after the performance.
ex.ko: 그는 공연 후 관객에게 허리를 굽혀 인사했다.

bow.en: a weapon used for shooting arrows, made of curved wood and string

ex.en: The hunter used a bow and arrow.

bow.en: a knot with two loops, often made from ribbon

ex.en: She tied her hair with a bow.

boxing.en: a sport in which two people fight using only their fists

ex.en: He took up boxing to stay fit.

bracelet.en: a piece of jewelry worn around the wrist

ex.en: She got a silver bracelet for her birthday.

brainstorming.en: a way of solving problems in which a group shares ideas freely

ex.en: We had a brainstorming session to plan the event.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 행사를 계획하려고 브레인스토밍 시간을 가졌다.

ex.en: He bought a brand-new laptop.

brass.en: a yellow metal made from copper and zinc

ex.en: The door handle is made of brass.

bravely.en: in a way that shows courage

ex.en: She bravely faced her fears.

breakthrough.en: an important discovery or development, especially after a lot of effort

ex.en: Scientists made a breakthrough in cancer research.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 암 연구에서 획기적인 발전을 이루었다.

breast.en: the front part of the body between the neck and the stomach

ex.en: He held the baby against his breast.

breath.en: the air that you take into and let out of your lungs

ex.en: Take a deep breath before you start.

breathless.en: unable to breathe easily, often after physical effort

ex.en: She was breathless after running up the stairs.
ex.ko: 그녀는 계단을 뛰어 올라간 후 숨이 찼다.

ex.en: Rabbits breed very quickly.

breed.en: a particular type of animal within a species

ex.en: What breed is your dog?

breeding.cue: 동물을 기르고 새끼를 낳게 하는 일
breeding.en: the process of keeping animals or plants to produce young ones

ex.en: The farm specializes in horse breeding.
ex.ko: 그 농장은 말 사육을 전문으로 한다.

brick.en: a rectangular block of baked clay used for building

ex.en: The house is made of red brick.
She gave a brief explanation of the plan.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 계획에 대해 간단히 설명했다.

brief.en: give someone the important information they need before doing something

ex.en: The manager briefed the team before the meeting.

brief.en: a document that gives the facts of a legal case, or a summary of instructions

ex.en: The lawyer prepared a brief for the judge.
ex.ko: 그 변호사는 판사를 위한 사건 적요서를 준비했다.

briefly.en: for a short time, or in a few words

ex.en: Let me briefly explain what happened.
ex.ko: 무슨 일이 있었는지 간단히 설명해 드릴게요.

ex.en: The stars shone brightly in the night sky.

broad.en: large in size from one side to the other

ex.en: He has broad shoulders.

broad.en: covering a wide range of things

ex.en: She has a broad knowledge of world history.
ex.ko: 그녀는 세계사에 대한 폭넓은 지식을 가지고 있다.

broadcast.en: send out a program on radio or television

ex.en: The match will be broadcast live tonight.
ex.ko: 그 경기는 오늘 밤 생중계로 방송될 것이다.

broadcast.en: a program that is sent out on radio or television

ex.en: We watched the news broadcast together.

broccoli.en: a green vegetable with a thick stem and tight clusters of small green buds

ex.en: She steamed some broccoli for dinner.

bronze.en: a reddish-brown metal made by mixing copper and tin

ex.en: The statue was cast in bronze.

bronze.en: a medal awarded for finishing in third place

ex.en: She won the bronze medal in swimming.

brotherhood.en: a feeling of friendship and support between people, as if they were brothers

ex.en: The war created a sense of brotherhood among the soldiers.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁은 병사들 사이에 형제애를 만들어냈다.

bubble.cue: 물이나 비눗물 속의 둥근 공기 방울
bubble.en: a small ball of air or gas inside a liquid or floating in the air

ex.en: Bubbles rose to the surface of the water.

bubble.en: a situation in which prices rise very quickly and then suddenly fall

ex.en: The housing bubble burst, and prices crashed.
He went camping with his buddies.

builder.en: a person or company whose job is to build houses and other buildings

ex.en: The builder finished the house in six months.

bull.en: an adult male of the cattle family

ex.en: The bull chased the farmer across the field.
ex.ko: 그 황소는 들판을 가로질러 농부를 쫓았다.

bullet.en: a small piece of metal fired from a gun

ex.en: The bullet missed him by inches.

bulletin.en: a short official statement or report, especially on the news

ex.en: A news bulletin interrupted the program.

bump.en: hit something accidentally, usually while moving

ex.en: She bumped her head on the shelf.
I bumped into an old friend at the mall.
ex.ko: 나는 쇼핑몰에서 옛 친구를 우연히 마주쳤다.

bunch.cue: 포도·꽃 등이 여러 개 묶인 것
bunch.en: a number of things of the same kind that grow or are fastened together

ex.en: She bought a bunch of bananas.
A bunch of us are going to the beach this weekend.
ex.ko: 우리 중 몇 명이 이번 주말에 해변에 갈 거야.

burden.en: give someone a difficult or heavy responsibility

ex.en: She didn't want to burden her friends with her problems.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 문제로 친구들에게 부담을 주고 싶지 않았다.

burden.en: a heavy load of responsibility, work, or worry

ex.en: Debt has become a huge burden for the family.
ex.ko: 빚은 그 가족에게 큰 부담이 되었다.

bureau.en: a government department or agency responsible for a particular area

ex.en: She works for the federal bureau of investigation.

bureau.en: a piece of furniture with drawers for storing clothes (American English)

ex.en: He put his socks in the top drawer of the bureau.
ex.ko: 그는 서랍장 맨 위 서랍에 양말을 넣었다.

burglar.en: a person who enters a building illegally in order to steal things

ex.en: The burglar broke in through a window.
Smoke rose from the burning building.

burning.en: extremely important and needing immediate attention

ex.en: There's one burning question everyone wants answered.
ex.ko: 모두가 답을 원하는 긴급한 질문이 하나 있다.

burst.en: break open suddenly, especially because of pressure from inside

ex.en: The balloon burst with a loud pop.
ex.ko: 그 풍선은 큰 소리를 내며 터졌다.

burst.en: a sudden strong show of energy, emotion, or activity

ex.en: She felt a burst of energy after the coffee.
ex.ko: 그녀는 커피를 마신 후 폭발적인 에너지를 느꼈다.

ex.en: He accidentally busted the old radio.

bust.en: cause a business or plan to fail completely

ex.en: The financial crisis busted many small companies.
ex.ko: 그 금융 위기는 많은 소기업들을 파산시켰다.

butcher.en: kill an animal for its meat

ex.en: The farm butchers its own pigs.

butcher.en: a person whose job is to sell meat

ex.en: She bought some lamb chops at the butcher.

buyer.en: a person who buys something, especially a house or a large amount of goods

ex.en: The buyer offered less than the asking price.
ex.ko: 그 구매자는 호가보다 낮은 가격을 제시했다.

cabbage.en: a round vegetable with thick green or purple leaves

ex.en: She made a salad with cabbage and carrots.

cabin.en: a small simple house, usually made of wood

ex.en: They spent the weekend at a cabin in the mountains.

cabin.en: a room for passengers or crew on a ship or aircraft

ex.en: The flight attendant checked the cabin before takeoff.

cage.en: a structure of bars or wire in which animals or birds are kept

ex.en: The parrot lives in a large cage.

calculate.en: work out a number or amount using mathematics

ex.en: She calculated the total cost of the trip.

calculation.en: the process of using numbers to work out an amount or answer

ex.en: His calculation showed they needed more money.
ex.ko: 그의 계산에 따르면 그들은 더 많은 돈이 필요했다.

calculator.en: a small machine used for doing mathematical calculations

ex.en: She used a calculator to check her answer.
The calf followed its mother across the field.
ex.ko: 그 송아지는 들판을 가로질러 어미를 따라갔다.

calf.en: the back part of the leg below the knee

ex.en: He pulled a muscle in his calf while running.

caller.en: a person who makes a phone call

ex.en: The caller asked to speak to the manager.
ex.ko: 전화 건 사람이 매니저와 통화하고 싶다고 했다.

ex.en: We had an unexpected caller this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 예상치 못한 방문객이 있었다.

ex.en: She stayed calm during the emergency.

calm.en: (of weather or water) without much wind or movement

ex.en: The sea was calm all morning.
He handled the crisis with remarkable calmness.
ex.ko: 그는 놀라운 침착함으로 그 위기를 처리했다.

camel.en: a large animal with one or two humps that lives in the desert

ex.en: They rode camels across the desert.
ex.ko: 그들은 사막을 가로질러 낙타를 타고 갔다.

campsite.en: a place where people can camp

ex.en: We found a nice campsite near the lake.
ex.ko: 우리는 호수 근처에서 좋은 캠핑장을 찾았다.

canal.en: a long, narrow waterway built for boats or irrigation

ex.en: Boats travel through the Panama Canal.

cancel.en: decide that a planned event will not happen

ex.en: They canceled the meeting due to bad weather.

cancer.en: a serious disease in which cells grow abnormally and can spread

ex.en: She was diagnosed with lung cancer.

candle.en: a stick of wax with a wick that is burned to give light

ex.en: She lit a candle on the birthday cake.

canned.en: sealed in a can to preserve it

ex.en: We stocked up on canned beans.

canteen.en: a place in a school, factory, or other institution where meals are served

ex.en: Students eat lunch in the canteen.

canteen.en: a container for carrying water, especially when hiking or camping

ex.en: He filled his canteen before the hike.

capable.en: having the ability or skill to do something

ex.en: She is capable of solving difficult problems.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어려운 문제를 해결할 능력이 있다.

capacity.en: the amount that something can hold or produce

ex.en: The stadium has a capacity of 50,000 people.
ex.ko: 그 경기장은 5만 명을 수용할 수 있다.

capacity.en: the ability to do or understand something

ex.en: He has a great capacity for hard work.
ex.ko: 그는 열심히 일할 수 있는 대단한 역량을 가지고 있다.

capsule.en: a small container holding a dose of medicine, meant to be swallowed

ex.en: Take one capsule twice a day.
ex.ko: 하루에 두 번 캡슐 하나씩 복용하세요.

capture.en: catch a person, animal, or place by force

ex.en: The soldiers captured the enemy base.

capture.en: successfully represent or record something in words, pictures, or film

ex.en: The photo captured the beauty of the sunset.
ex.ko: 그 사진은 노을의 아름다움을 잘 담아냈다.

career.en: the series of jobs a person has in a particular area of work over their life

ex.en: She had a long career as a teacher.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교사로서 오랜 경력을 가지고 있다.

careless.en: not paying enough attention to what you are doing

ex.en: He made a careless mistake on the test.
She carelessly left the door unlocked.
ex.ko: 그녀는 부주의하게 문을 잠그지 않은 채 두었다.

carelessness.en: the quality of not paying enough attention

ex.en: The accident was caused by carelessness.

carpet.en: a thick fabric covering used for floors

ex.en: They laid a new carpet in the living room.

carriage.en: one of the separate sections of a train where passengers sit (British English)

ex.en: We sat in the first carriage of the train.
ex.ko: 우리는 기차의 첫 번째 객차에 앉았다.

carriage.en: a vehicle with wheels that is pulled by horses

ex.en: The princess arrived in a golden carriage.
ex.ko: 그 공주는 황금 마차를 타고 도착했다.

carton.en: a container made of cardboard or plastic, used for holding food or drink

ex.en: She bought a carton of milk.

cashpoint.en: a machine in a wall that lets you take out money with a card (British English)

ex.en: He withdrew some cash from the cashpoint.
ex.ko: 그는 현금 인출기에서 돈을 좀 뽑았다.

ex.en: He wore casual clothes to the party.
ex.ko: 그는 파티에 편안한 평상복을 입고 갔다.

casual.en: happening occasionally or without a fixed schedule

ex.en: She works a casual job on weekends.

categorize.en: put things into groups according to their type

ex.en: The books are categorized by genre.

category.en: a group of things that share similar features

ex.en: This movie fits into the comedy category.

cattle.en: cows and bulls kept on a farm

ex.en: The farmer raises cattle for milk and meat.
ex.ko: 그 농부는 우유와 고기를 위해 소를 기른다.

caution.en: great care taken to avoid danger or mistakes

ex.en: Handle the chemicals with caution.

cautious.en: careful to avoid danger or mistakes

ex.en: Be cautious when driving in the rain.

cave.en: a large hole in the side of a hill or under the ground

ex.en: They explored a cave near the coast.

CD-ROM.en: a compact disc used for storing computer data

ex.en: The software came on a CD-ROM.
